bibliographic_data.title: Transformation of topological optical states via spiral modulation in fractional-diffraction systems
bibliographic_data.abstract: We propose a scheme for manipulations of a variety of topological states in fractional optical systems through spiral modulation of the local refraction index. An analytical approximation, based on a truncated finite-mode system, and direct simulations reveal that the spiral modulation supports direct mutual conversion between eigenmodes with topological-charge difference Δm = 1, driven by the resonant coupling between the modes and the underlying spiral modulation. The conversion between eigenmodes with Δm = 2 requires involvement of an intermediary mode and precise tuning of the resonance condition. We further explore the conversion of degenerate modes under the action of azimuthal modulation. Modulated degenerate modes exhibit incomplete conversion, evolving into intermediate states with odd parity symmetry. Finally, we examine nonlinear effects on the spiral-modulation-induced mode conversion, identifying an essential nonlinearity-induced resonance-frequency shift that critically affects the conversion efficiency.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a scheme for manipulating topological optical states in fractional systems through spiral modulation of the local refraction index, demonstrating direct and indirect conversion between eigenmodes with specific topological charges.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors use an analytical approximation based on a truncated finite-mode system and direct simulations to study the effects of spiral modulation on eigenmodes. They also explore the impact of nonlinearities on mode conversion.
outcomes: Demonstrated direct mutual conversion between eigenmodes with topological-charge difference Δm = 1
Showed that conversion between eigenmodes with Δm = 2 requires an intermediary mode and precise tuning of the resonance condition
Explored the conversion of degenerate modes under azimuthal modulation, resulting in incomplete conversion into intermediate states with odd parity symmetry
Identified nonlinearity-induced resonance-frequency shift affecting the efficiency of spiral-modulation-induced mode conversion
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper explores the manipulation of topological optical states using fractional systems. It proposes a scheme involving spiral modulation of the local refraction index to convert between eigenmodes with specific topological charges. The authors use analytical approximations and simulations to demonstrate direct mutual conversion for Δm = 1, while indirect conversions are required for Δm = 2. They also investigate the effects of nonlinearities on these conversions, noting significant shifts in resonance frequencies.
